prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `505fff84`.
codex_response:
Implemented task `505fff84`. The rule is: for each input row containing both `1` and `8`, take the cells strictly between them and stack those strips in top-to-bottom row order. I used the Opus summary hint because it matched the official examples; there was no Sonnet entry, so no `notes.md` was needed. No new primitive was added.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_505fff84/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_505fff84/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_505fff84/generator.py). The generator stays in-family by building `0/2` noise grids, choosing a common output strip width and marked-row set, and then inserting `1 ... 8` marker pairs with varying left offsets so the output strips are the latent structure. Validation passed end to end: `6/6` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, and I opened the preview sheet to sanity-check the visuals.
